title: Benthamia angustifolia
taxonomy: Benthamia angustifolia is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a genus of orchids native to Madagascar. Within the Orchidaceae family, Benthamia angustifolia is placed in the subfamily Vanilloideae. This subfamily includes about 40 genera of orchids, primarily found in tropical regions of the world. Benthamia angustifolia is further classified into the tribe Vanilleae, which contains about 20 genera of orchids.

intro: Benthamia angustifolia, also known as the slender benthamia, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Benthamia angustifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Benthamia angustifolia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make tonics, teas, and other herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
